foreignObject.h5:div: Ever since I started programming at around 14 years old I have been fascinated by interactively
exploring ideas and finding problems to my mediocre solutions. The projects coming out of that
were slowly but almost always for sure forgotten over time, even by myself. In the last few
weeks I figured that to be a real shame and that I should start writing down my ideas and
solutions to them, especially because technical writing is a skill I enjoy and want to improve.
Because I don’t do things by halves, I intent to reignite my online persona
to cover all these technical/semi-professional aspects of my life. It shall be used as a central
point of reference for myself, including videos on
as well as (probably very unregular) blog posts on
Now, there is one problem: The website does not yet exist. So let’s go build it. Let’s use
But why not use Markdown or some of the 1000 blog static site generators out there?
Very good question. There are a few reasons for that, most importantly:
Writing code is fun and with our custom implementation only our imagination
creativity is none of my strengths)
Furthermore, I really enjoy Typst for its capabilities and yet simplicity. It is a joy to write and the
results are beautiful. Show me where Markdown can do this:

It is very easy to style and with correct abuse can be used to represent the central source of
truth for the whole website’s look and feel. More on that in

to create a React component that would render the blog post. It is a fantastic

How to make responsive in website
Build multiple variants of the same document with different widths and themes
Delta compress all of them with custom xdelta3 bindings to arbitary reference
Store these compressed files as base64 in the static website (handled by server
